title: drimia pygmaea
intro: Drimia pygmaea, also known as the dwarf drimia or small drimia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is a bulbous perennial that has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and snake bites. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it beneficial for reducing swelling and fighting off infections, thus providing health benefits such as relief from pain and inflammation. Drimia pygmaea can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments.
